elements.sent-2411-001: Cohen-Ramanujan sum, denoted by $c_r^s(n)$, is an exponential sum similar to the Ramanujan sum $c_r(n):=\sum\limits_{\substack{h=1 {(h,r)=1 ^{r e^{\frac{2\pi i n h {r }$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-002: An arithmetical function $f$ is said to admit a Cohen-Ramanujan expansion $ f(n):=\sum\limits_{r}\widehat{f (r)c_r^s(n)$ if the series on the right hand side converges for suitable complex numbers $\widehat{f (r)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-003: Given two arithmetical functions $f$ and $g$ with absolutely convergent Cohen-Ramanujan expansions, we derive an asymptotic formula for the sum $\sum\limits_{\substack{n\leq N f(n)g(n+h)$ where $h$ is a fixed non negative integer.}}
elements.sent-2411-004: We also provide Cohen-Ramanujan expansions for certain functions to illustrate some of the results we prove consequently.
elements.sent-2411-005: Srinivasa Ramanujan introduced the exponential sum \begin{equation}c_r(n) =\sum\limits_{\substack{{m=1} (m,r)=1}}^{r}e^{\frac{2 \pi imn}{r}}\label{ramanujan_sum}\end{equation} in \cite{ramanujan1918certain and used this sum to derive infinite Fourier series like expansions for several arithmetical functions in the form $\sum\limits_{r a_rc_r(n)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-006: The exponential sum \eqref{ramanujan_sum now known as the Ramanujan sum has several interesting properties, many of which can be found in \cite{tom1976introduction , \cite{mccarthy2012introduction , or \cite{sivaramakrishnan1988classical .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-007: If a Fourier series like expansion in the form $\sum\limits_{r a_rc_r(n)$ exists for an arithmetical function, the function is said to possess a Ramanujan-Fourier series expansion or simply a Ramanujan expansion.}
elements.sent-2411-008: Ramanujan expansions were computed for various functions by many other authors, see, for example \cite{hardy1921note .}
elements.sent-2411-009: Various conditions for the existence of Ramanujan expansions appeared in papers like \cite{hardy1921note , \cite{lucht2010survey and \cite{murty2013ramanujan .}}}
elements.sent-2411-010: Ramanujan sum has been generalized in many directions.
elements.sent-2411-012: Cohen gave a generalization defining \begin{equation}\label{gen-ram-sum} c_r^s(n) :=\sum\limits_{\substack{h=1 {(h,r^s)_s=1}}}^{r^s}e^{\frac{2\pi i n h}{r^s}},\end{equation} where for a positive integer $s$, integers $m,n$, not both zero, the generalized GCD of $m$ and $n$ denoted by $(m,n)_{s}$ is the largest $l^s$ (where $l\in \N$) dividing both $m$ and $n$.
elements.sent-2411-013: If $(m,n)_s=1$, then $m$ and $n$ are said to be relatively $s$-prime to each other.
elements.sent-2411-014: When $s=1$, generalized GCD becomes the usual GCD and then the sum (\ref{gen-ram-sum ) reduces to the usual Ramanujan sum.}
elements.sent-2411-015: We will refer to the sum \eqref{gen-ram-sum by the name Cohen-Ramanujan sum.}
elements.sent-2411-016: Like the usual Ramanujan sum, for certain specific values of $s$ and $n$, this sum becomes equal to various well known arithmetical functions.
elements.sent-2411-017: For example, $c_r^s(0) = \Phi_s(r^s)=J_s(r)$, where $\Phi_s$ is the Klee's function and $J_s$ is the Jordan totient function.
elements.sent-2411-018: Please see the next section for the definitions of these two functions.
elements.sent-2411-019: Cohen derived various properties of this sum and proved several results using this generalization in a series of papers \cite{cohen1949extension , \cite{cohen1955extensionofr , and \cite{cohen1956extension .}}}
elements.sent-2411-020: Another generalization of the Ramanujan sum, and a list of references to some more generalizations can be found in \cite{subba1966new .}
elements.sent-2411-021: Recently, Haukkanen and McCarthy gave another generalization in \cite{haukkanen1991sums .}
elements.sent-2411-022: An arithmetical function $f$ is said to admit a Cohen-Ramanujan expansion \begin{equation}f(n):=\sum\limits_{r}\widehat{f}(r)c_r^s(n),\end{equation} if the series on the right hand side converges for suitable complex numbers $\widehat{f (r)$.}
elements.sent-2411-023: A study on the Cohen-Ramanujan expansions of some arithmetical functions were conducted by these authors in \cite{chandran2023ramanujan .}
elements.sent-2411-024: Some necessary and sufficient conditions for the existence of such expansions were also given there.
elements.sent-2411-025: To the best of our knowledge, such series expansions are not available for any other generalization of the Ramanujan sum as of now.
elements.sent-2411-026: In \cite{hardy1921note , Hardy derived a Ramanujan expansion for the function $\Lambda_1(n): = (\phi(n)/n)\Lambda(n)$ in the form \begin{equation}\Lambda_1(n) = \sum\limits_{q = 1}^{\infty} \frac{\mu(q)}{\phi(q)} c_q(n)\label{hardyexp},\end{equation} where $\Lambda(n)$ is the von Mangoldt function defined as \begin{equation}\Lambda(n) :=  \log p, if $n = p^k$ , $p$ is prime and $k$ any positive integer 0,  otherwise. \end{equation} and $\mu$ is the M{\"o bius function.}}
elements.formula-2411-005: \begin{equation}\Lambda(n) :=  \log p, if $n = p^k$ , $p$ is prime and $k$ any positive integer 0,  otherwise. \end{equation}

H.}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: Padma \cite{gadiyar1999ramanujan showed that Hardy-Littlewood conjecture on twin primes can be proved if the Wienerâ€“Khintchine theorem can be applied to the Ramanujan expansion of the arithmetical function $\Lambda_1$ provided that the expansion is uniformly and absolutely convergent.}
elements.sent-2411-030: They derived the formula \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{n \leq N} \Lambda(n) \Lambda(n+h) \sim N \sum\limits_{\substack{r = 1}}^{\infty}\frac{\mu^2(r)}{\phi(r)}c_r(h),\end{equation} which agrees with the Hardy-Littlewood conjecture.
elements.sent-2411-031: But note that this identity is valid only if the Ramanujan expansion \eqref{hardyexp derived by Hardy is absolutely convergent, which is not actually the case.}

elements.sent-2411-034: Padma \cite{gopalakrishna2014ramanujan derived an asymptotic formula for the sum $\sum\limits_{n\leq N f(n)g(n+h),$ where $f$ and $g$ are two arithmetical functions with absolutely convergent Ramanujan expansions.}}
elements.sent-2411-035: In this paper, we use the Cohen-Ramanujan expansion in the context initiated in \cite{gopalakrishna2014ramanujan and derive an asymptotic formula for the sum $\sum\limits_{n\leq N f(n)g(n+h)$, where $f$ and $g$ are arithmetical functions with absolutely convergent Cohen-Ramanujan expansions satisfying some additional conditions.}}
elements.sent-2411-036: Therefore, our results extend the results in \cite{gopalakrishna2014ramanujan .}
elements.sent-2411-037: We also provide some examples to demonstrate our results.
elements.sent-2411-038: We also give a condition for the existence of the Cohen-Ramanujan expansion for $f_h(n) := f(n+h)$ when such an expansion for $f$ exists.
elements.sent-2411-039: The main results we propose in this paper are the following.
elements.sent-2411-041: Then as $N$ tends to infinity, $\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N f(n)g(n)\sim N \sum\limits_{\substack{r \widehat{f (r)\widehat{g (r) \Phi_s(r^s)$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: Then as $N$ tends to infinity, $\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N f(n)g(n+h)\sim N \sum\limits_{\substack{r \widehat{f (r)\widehat{g (r) c_r^s(h)$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-044: Then $f_h$ has an absolutely convergent Cohen-Ramanujan expansion with coefficients $\dfrac{\widehat{f (r)c_r^s(h) {\Phi_s(r^s)}$.. \end{theo.}}}
elements.sent-2411-045: Most of the notations, functions and identities we use in this paper are standard and their definitions can be found in \cite{tom1976introduction or \cite{mccarthy2012introduction .}}
elements.sent-2411-046: However, for the sake of completeness, we include the most important ones below.
elements.sent-2411-047: As usual, $[m, n]$ denotes the lcm of integers $m,n$.
elements.sent-2411-048: As in the case of the well known identity $(m,n)[m,n]=mn$, it is easy to verify that $ (m^s,n^s)_s [m^s,n^s]=m^sn^s$ where $(m,n)_s$ denotes the generalized GCD of $m$ and $n$.
elements.sent-2411-049: An integer $m$ is said to be an $s^{th$ power free integer if for no positive integer $k>1$ exists such that $k^s|m$.}
elements.sent-2411-050: If $(m, n)_s = 1$, then we say that $m$ and $n$ are relatively $s$-prime to each other.
elements.sent-2411-051: The subset $N$ of a complete residue system $M (\text{mod n^s)$ consisting of all elements of $M$ that are relatively $s$-prime to $n^s$ is called an $s$-reduced residue system (mod $n$).}
elements.sent-2411-052: For positive integers $s$ and $n$, the Jordan totient function $J_s(n)$ is defined to give the number of ordered sets of $s$ elements from a complete residue system (mod $n$) such that the greatest common divisor of each set is prime to $n$.
elements.sent-2411-053: The Klee's function $\Phi_{s}$ is defined to give the cardinality of the set $\{m\in\N : 1\leq m\leq n, (m,n)_s=1\}$.
elements.sent-2411-054: Note that $\Phi_1 = \varphi$, the Euler totient function.
elements.sent-2411-055: It is known that \cite[Section V.3]{sivaramakrishnan1988classical $n^s = \sum\limits_{d|n J_s(d)$ and $J_s(n) = \Phi_s(n^s)$.. Hence \begin{equation}\label{phi-reltn} n^s = \sum\limits_{d|n}\Phi_s(d^s) =\sum\limits_{d^s|n^s}\Phi_s(d^s).\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-056: By $\tau_{s (n)$, where $s,n\in \N$, we mean the number of positive integers $l^s$ dividing $n$ where $l\in \N$.}
elements.sent-2411-058: We denote the distance from $x$ to the nearest integer by $\Vert x \Vert$.
elements.sent-2411-059: For $x \in \R$, $\llbracket x\rrbracket$ will be used to denote the largest integer $n$ such that $n \leq x$.
elements.sent-2411-060: The next theorem is essential in estalishing the main results that we prove in this paper.

elements.sent-2411-093: Therefore, each class modulo $[r^s,k^s]$ is repeated atmost $d^s$ times.
elements.sent-2411-094: Then we get, \begin{equation}\left\vert \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} \sum\limits_{\substack{a=1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s}\sum\limits_{\substack{b=1 (b,k^s)_s=1}}^{k^s}e^{\frac{2\pi i bh}{ks}}e^{2\pi i \left(\frac{ak^s+br^s}{r^sk^s}\right)n} \right\vert \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{a=1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s}\sum\limits_{\substack{b=1 (b,k^s)_s=1}}^{k^s} \frac{1}{\left\Vert \frac{ak^s+br^s}{r^sk^s}\right\Vert} \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{a=1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s}\sum\limits_{\substack{b=1 (b,k^s)_s=1}}^{k^s} \frac{1}{\left\Vert\frac{ak_1^s+br_1^s}{[r^s,k^s]}\right\Vert} \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{ak^s+br^s=1}}^{r^sk^s} \frac{1}{\left\Vert\frac{ak_1^s+br_1^s}{[r^s,k^s]}\right\Vert}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-095: Since $(r^s,k^s)_s[r^s,k^s]=r^sk^s, r^s=d^sr_1^s, k^s = d^s k_1^s$ and $d^s = (r^s,k^s)_s$ we get the last step above to be equal to \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{ak^s+br^s=1}}^{r^sk^s} \frac{1}{\left\Vert\frac{ak_1^s+br_1^s}{d^sr_1^sk_1^s}\right\Vert} = \sum\limits_{\substack{t=1}}^{r^sk^s} \frac{1}{\left\Vert\frac{t}{d^sr_1^sk_1^s}\right\Vert} \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{t=1}}^{r^sk^s} \frac{1}{\frac{t}{d^sr_1^sk_1^s}} = {d^sr_1^sk_1^s} \sum\limits_{\substack{t=1}}^{r^sk^s} \frac{1}{t}\end{equation} Now by by Theorem \ref{l1 , we get the last step to be equal to \begin{equation}{d^sr_1^sk_1^s} \Big( \log(r^sk^s)+C+O(r^sk^s)\Big) \ll [r^s,k^s] \log(r^sk^s) \ll r^sk^s \log(r^sk^s).\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-096: By the Cauchy-Schwartz inequality $\left(\sum\limits_{\substack{i=1 ^{n u_iv_i\right)^2\leq \left(\sum\limits_{\substack{i=1 ^{n u_i\right)^2 \left(\sum\limits_{\substack{i=1 ^{n v_i\right)^2$, we have \begin{equation}{\vert \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} c_r^s(n)c_k^s(n+h)\vert }^2 \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} {\vert c_r^s(n) \vert}^2\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} {\vert c_k^s(n+h) \vert}^2.\end{equation}.}}}}}}}}}
elements.formula-2411-025: \begin{equation}{\vert \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} c_r^s(n)c_k^s(n+h)\vert }^2 \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} {\vert c_r^s(n) \vert}^2\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} {\vert c_k^s(n+h) \vert}^2.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-097: If we proceed as in the proof of Lemma \ref{lem1 , we can see that \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} {\vert c_r^s(n) \vert}^2 \leq N r^s(\tau_s(r^s))^2\end{equation} and \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} {\vert c_k^s(n+h) \vert}^2 \leq (N+ h ) k^s(\tau_s(k^s))^2.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-098: From this, we get \begin{equation}{\vert \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} c_r^s(n)c_k^s(n+h)\vert }^2 \leq N r^s \tau_s(r^s)^2 (N+ h)k^s \tau_s(k^s)^2.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-099: Now we prove the first theorem we stated in the introduction.

elements.sent-2411-102: The second sum will be over $rk>U$.. So \begin{equation}\label{equ-4} \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} f(n)g(n) = \sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n)\nonumber +\sum\limits_{\substack{rk> U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n).\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-103: Now by Lemma \ref{lem2 , we get \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k) \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n) = \sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)(\delta_{r,k} N c_r^{s}(0)+O(r^sk^s \log r^sk^s) =\sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)(\delta_{r,k} N \Phi_s(r^s)+O(r^sk^s \log r^sk^s) = N\sum\limits_{\substack{r^2 \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(r)\Phi_s(r^s)+\sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)O(r^sk^s \log r^sk^s).\end{equation} Now $\vert c_r^s(n)\vert \leq \sigma_{1,s (n)$.}}
elements.formula-2411-030: \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k) \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n) = \sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)(\delta_{r,k} N c_r^{s}(0)+O(r^sk^s \log r^sk^s) =\sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)(\delta_{r,k} N \Phi_s(r^s)+O(r^sk^s \log r^sk^s) = N\sum\limits_{\substack{r^2 \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(r)\Phi_s(r^s)+\sum\limits_{\substack{rk \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)O(r^sk^s \log r^sk^s).\end{equation}

elements.sent-2411-107: By Lemma \ref{lem1 , the second sum in Equation (\ref{equ-4 ) satisfies the inequality \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{rk > U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n) \leq N \sum\limits_{\substack{rk > U}}\vert \widehat{f}(r)\vert \vert\widehat{g}(k)\vert \tau_s(r^s) \tau_s(k^s) (r^s,k^s)_s.\end{equation} Since $\sum\limits_{\substack{r,k }\vert \widehat{f (r)\vert \vert\widehat{g (k)\vert \tau_s(r^s) \tau_s(k^s) (r^s,k^s)_s < \infty$, we have \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{rk > U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k)\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n) \ll N.\end{equation}.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-109: Thus Equation (\ref{equ-4 ) gives \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} f(n)g(n) = N \sum\limits_{\substack{r^2 \leq U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(r) \Phi_s(r^s)+O(U^s \log U^s)+O(N)\end{equation} which is equal to \begin{equation}N \sum\limits_{\substack{r=1}}^{\infty}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(r) \Phi_s(r^s) - N \sum\limits_{\substack{r^2 > U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(r) \Phi_s(r^s) + O(U^s \log U^s).\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-117: Now we consider the absolute value of the second sum in Equation \eqref{eq:fn-gn-sum .. \begin{equation}\vert \sum\limits_{\substack{rk > U}}\widehat{f}(r) \widehat{g}(k) \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n+h) \vert \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{rk > U}}\vert\widehat{f}(r)\vert \vert\widehat{g}(k)\left| \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}} c_r^{s}(n)c_k^{s}(n+h) \right| \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{rk > U}}\vert\widehat{f}(r)\vert \vert\widehat{g}(k)\vert N^{\frac{1}{2}}(N+\vert h \vert)^{\frac{1}{2}} (r^sk^s)^{\frac{1}{2}} \tau_s(r^s) \tau_s(k^s) \text{ by Lemma \ref{lem3}} \leq N^{\frac{1}{2}}(N+\vert h \vert)^{\frac{1}{2}} \sum\limits_{\substack{rk > U}}\vert\widehat{f}(r)\vert \vert\widehat{g}(k)\vert (r^sk^s)^{\frac{1}{2}} \tau_s(r^s) \tau_s(k^s).\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-127: Now we proceed to discuss the existence of Cohen-Ramanujan expansion for $f_h$ derived from $f$.
elements.sent-2411-128: Consider the sum \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^s(n)c_k^s(n+h) = \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}\sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}}\sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k d^s\mid n+h}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s = \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N d^s \mid n+h}} 1\end{equation} \begin{equation}= \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \sum\limits_{\substack{n+h \leq N+h d^s \mid n+h}} 1 = \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \left\llbracket\frac{N+h}{d^s}\right\rrbracket \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \frac{N+h}{d^s} \leq (N+h) \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} 1 \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} 1 \leq (N+h) \Phi_s(r^s) \tau(k) \leq 2N \Phi_s(r^s) \tau(k).\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-045: \begin{equation}\sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}c_r^s(n)c_k^s(n+h) = \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N}}\sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}}\sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k d^s\mid n+h}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s = \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq N d^s \mid n+h}} 1\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-046: \begin{equation}= \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \sum\limits_{\substack{n+h \leq N+h d^s \mid n+h}} 1 = \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \left\llbracket\frac{N+h}{d^s}\right\rrbracket \leq \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} e^{\frac{2 \pi i an}{r^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} \mu\left(\frac{k}{d}\right) d^s \frac{N+h}{d^s} \leq (N+h) \sum\limits_{\substack{a = 1 (a,r^s)_s=1}}^{r^s} 1 \sum\limits_{\substack{d \mid k}} 1 \leq (N+h) \Phi_s(r^s) \tau(k) \leq 2N \Phi_s(r^s) \tau(k).\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-129: The orthogonality relation allows us to write down the possible candidates for the Cohen-Ramanujan coefficients of any given arithmetical function.
elements.sent-2411-130: Indeed, if $f(n) = \sum\limits_{\substack{q = 1 ^{\infty}\widehat{f (q)c_q^s(n)$ , then $f(n)c_r^s(n) = \sum\limits_{\substack{q = 1 ^{\infty}\widehat{f (q)c_q^s(n)c_r^s(n).$ So we get, \begin{equation}\lim_{x^s \to \infty} \frac{1}{x^s} \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq x^s}} f(n) c_r^s(n) = \lim_{x^s \to \infty} \frac{1}{x^s} \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq x^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{q = 1}}^{\infty}\widehat{f}(q)c_q^s(n) c_r^s(n) = \sum\limits_{\substack{q = 1}}^{\infty}\widehat{f}(q) \lim_{x^s \to \infty} \frac{1}{x^s} \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq x^s}} c_q^s(n) c_r^s(n) = \widehat{f}(r) \lim_{x^s \to \infty} \Phi_s(r^s).\end{equation}.}}}}}}
elements.formula-2411-047: \begin{equation}\lim_{x^s \to \infty} \frac{1}{x^s} \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq x^s}} f(n) c_r^s(n) = \lim_{x^s \to \infty} \frac{1}{x^s} \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq x^s}} \sum\limits_{\substack{q = 1}}^{\infty}\widehat{f}(q)c_q^s(n) c_r^s(n) = \sum\limits_{\substack{q = 1}}^{\infty}\widehat{f}(q) \lim_{x^s \to \infty} \frac{1}{x^s} \sum\limits_{\substack{n \leq x^s}} c_q^s(n) c_r^s(n) = \widehat{f}(r) \lim_{x^s \to \infty} \Phi_s(r^s).\end{equation}
